attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches in final 100-250m, preferring to sit 3rd-5th wheel through final kilometer and time acceleration latest. Centers on clean road lines rather than edges when launching.
team_tactics: Deceuninck/Soudal Quick-Step deploys late disruption attacks inside 10km to create positioning chaos favoring him, with teammates (Gilbert, Lampaert) providing 1-1.5km leadouts. Team shows flexibility to substitute Ballerini when his form falters.
terrain_preference: Pure flat finishes, technical/chaotic sprints, and straight 9km boulevards. Exposed by even moderate late gradients, lumpy circuits, and rolling terrain that drops pure sprinters before finale.
vulnerability: Vulnerable to equipment mechanicals inside final 2km despite perfect positioning. Struggles with positioning when boxed in tight finales. Gets dropped on minor climbs and rolling roads. Form deteriorated significantly by 2023 mid-season.
key_patterns: Wins from chaos when structured leadout trains collapse; capitalizes when rivals' plans disintegrate (4 instances)
Excels in technical/wet finishes requiring patience; waits longest before launching sprint (5+ instances)
Deliberately skips intermediate sprints to conserve energy for stage finale; superior race-craft execution (2 instances)
Positioning errors in final 500m when too far back; struggles despite team control (3 instances)
Peaks better in multi-stage blocks than one-offs; multiple wins within single race weeks (2018-2021 pattern)
2023 form collapse: dropped early, abandoned chases, positioning issues after strong 2021-2022 comeback
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 showed dramatic decline—dropped before breakaways established at Tour, used barriers aggressively suggesting positioning desperation, team substituted Ballerini. Peak form delayed versus 2022 trajectory when he had 11 wins.
